behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality (grammar/meaning preservation). A stubbed translator would hide this class of failure. Not Workflow because this behavior is a single model-output quality check, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run real translation API calls on a multilingual eval set and assert semantic adequacy/fluency thresholds (e.g., human-rated or reference-based quality gates) to catch meaning-loss or literal word-for-word outputs.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration (API error path -> fallback translator -> response shaping). Real model output is irrelevant; 503 can be injected by a deterministic stub. Rejected System: behavior text only requires handling a 503 status, not real network/OS failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client stub (returns 503) + SQLite-backed fallback module; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no surfaced error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at service/repository/SQLite boundary (query, schema, mapping), not just pure function logic. Rejected System: behavior text specifies local SQLite lookup only, no real external network/subprocess dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local SQLite test DB seeded with known phrase->translation rows; call fallback path and assert exact stored translation is returned for matching phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Although edit-distance math alone is unit-testable, this behavior explicitly includes persistence ('stores the metric'), so real failure boundary is compute + storage wiring. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about deterministic distance computation/storage, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide fixed primary/fallback strings with known distance, execute quality-tracking path, and assert persisted metric value in DB equals expected distance.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration and async side-effect behavior across local modules (selection policy + background logging). Rejected Agentic: behavior text checks source-selection/logging behavior, not whether model translation quality is correct.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators stubbed as available and deterministic outputs, assert response equals primary output immediately, then assert async quality-comparison log/record is eventually written.
